docs.title: Lite-Anvil - Lightweight Code Editor Built in Rust
Lite-Anvil
docs.text:
A lightweight, lightning fast, and powerful code editor built in Rust, with Lua for user plugins.

Get Started View on GitHub

docs.title: Built-in LSP
docs.text: 25+ languages with diagnostics, completion, hover, go-to-definition, references, rename, code actions, formatting, inlay hints, semantic highlighting, and call/type hierarchy.

docs.title: Embedded Terminal
docs.text: Full PTY terminal with ANSI colors, scrollback, color schemes. Open bottom, left, right, or as a tab.

docs.title: Integrated Test Runner
docs.text: Auto-detects Cargo, npm/vitest/jest, pytest, Go, dotnet, Gradle, Maven, sbt, PHPUnit, Make. Run all tests or the current file.

docs.title: Git Integration
docs.text: Branch/status in status bar, tree highlighting, diff views, stage/unstage, commit, push, pull, stash.

docs.title: 50+ Syntax Grammars
docs.text: Rust, Go, Python, TypeScript, C/C++, Java, Kotlin, Scala, F#, C#, Haskell, Zig, Elixir, Erlang, OCaml, Gleam, Dart, Swift, Ruby, and many more.

docs.title: Fast & Lightweight
docs.text: Native Rust core. Sub-second startup. Low memory footprint. All core modules, views, commands, and bundled plugins are pure Rust via mlua.

docs.title: Multi-Cursor Editing
docs.text: Ctrl+D to add next occurrence, Ctrl+Shift+L for all occurrences, Ctrl+Alt+L to turn find matches into cursors.

docs.title: Project Workspace Memory
docs.text: Open files, tabs, splits, and scroll positions restore when switching between projects.

docs.title: Bookmarks & Indent Guides
docs.text: Toggle line bookmarks (Ctrl+F2), navigate with F2. Vertical indent guides at each level. Line sorting, unique, reverse.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Lite-Anvil is a fork of Lite XL, rewritten from the ground up in Rust. The core, all views, commands, and bundled plugins are native Rust. User plugins and configuration remain Lua for easy extensibility.

Languages 50+ syntax grammars, 25+ built-in LSP configurations Platform Linux, macOS, Windows License MIT Rust version 1.85+

docs.title: About
docs.text: Lite-Anvil is a lightweight code editor forked from Lite XL, migrated to Rust, then extended and modified significantly.

docs.title: Origins
docs.text: Lite XL is a C-based lightweight editor inspired by lite. Lite-Anvil replaced the C core and most of the Lua code with Rust while keeping Lua as the user plugin language via mlua.

docs.title: License
docs.text:
MIT -- see LICENSE.

Font: Lilex

Documentation: Built with Material for MkDocs by Martin Donath.

docs.title: Author
docs.text: Dan Pozmanter

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Lite-Anvil exposes a native Rust API to Lua plugins. The annotation files in docs/api/ contain EmmyLua annotations for use with LSP servers like lua-language-server. Point your LSP at that directory to get autocompletion and type checking when writing plugins or editing config.lua.

docs.title: Native Modules
docs.text: Module Description Source system File system, clipboard, window management, events system.lua renderer Drawing primitives, font loading and measurement renderer.lua regex PCRE2 regular expressions regex.lua process Child process spawning and stream I/O process.lua renwindow Window creation and persistence renwindow.lua dirmonitor File system change monitoring dirmonitor.lua utf8extra UTF-8 string utilities utf8extra.lua string (u* extensions) UTF-8 methods injected into the string table string.lua

docs.title: Globals
docs.text: All global variables set by the runtime (ARGS, SCALE, PLATFORM, USERDIR, DATADIR, PATHSEP, VERSION, etc.) are documented in globals.lua.

docs.title: Core Modules
docs.text: These modules are implemented in Rust but exposed as standard require-able Lua modules:

docs.title: Application
docs.text: Module Description core Application lifecycle, threads, logging, projects, file dialogs core.command Command registry (add, perform, predicates) core.keymap Keybinding management core.config Editor settings and plugin configuration core.style Colors, fonts, theme registration core.common Utility functions (paths, fuzzy match, serialize, colors) core.storage Persistent key-value storage across restarts

docs.title: Document
docs.text: Module Description core.doc Document model (buffer, selections, undo/redo) core.doc.translate Cursor movement helpers core.doc.search Text search with regex/plain/wrap support core.syntax Syntax grammar registry

docs.title: UI
docs.text: Module Description core.object OOP base class (extend, new, is, extends) core.view Base UI view class core.docview Code editor view core.commandview Command palette core.contextmenu Right-click menus core.nagview Confirmation dialogs core.scrollbar Scrollbar component core.statusview Status bar core.logview Log viewer

docs.title: System
docs.text:
Module Description core.dirwatch Directory change watcher core.process Process streams with coroutine-aware I/O core.project Project file listing and filtering core.gitignore .gitignore pattern matching core.regex Regex helpers (find, match, find_offsets) core.ime Input method editor hooks core.plugin_api Stable facade for plugin authors

For full details, see the Plugin Guide.

docs.title: General
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+P Command palette Ctrl+O Open file (supports file:42 to go to line) Ctrl+Shift+O Open file from project Ctrl+N New file Ctrl+S Save (save-as for unnamed files) Ctrl+W Close tab Ctrl+Q Quit Ctrl+= / Ctrl+- Font zoom in / out F10 Toggle line wrapping

docs.title: Editing
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+D Add next occurrence to selection Ctrl+Shift+L Select all occurrences Ctrl+Alt+L Turn find matches into multi-cursors Ctrl+/ Toggle line comment Ctrl+Shift+Up/Down Move line up/down Ctrl+Shift+D Duplicate line Alt+Shift+F Format document (LSP)

docs.title: Find & Replace
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+F Find in file Ctrl+H Replace in file Alt+S Toggle find-in-selection F3 / Shift+F3 Next / previous match

docs.title: Bookmarks
docs.text: Key Action Ctrl+F2 Toggle bookmark F2 Next bookmark Shift+F2 Previous bookmark

docs.title: LSP
docs.text: Key Action F12 Go to definition Ctrl+F12 Go to type definition Shift+F12 Find references F2 Rename symbol Ctrl+Space Trigger completion Ctrl+Shift+Space Signature help Ctrl+K Hover Ctrl+T Document symbols Ctrl+Alt+T Workspace symbols Ctrl+Shift+A Code action Alt+Return Quick fix F8 / Shift+F8 Next / previous diagnostic Alt+F12 Incoming calls Ctrl+Shift+F12 Outgoing calls Alt+F11 Supertypes Ctrl+Shift+F11 Subtypes

docs.title: Test Runner
docs.text:
Key Action Ctrl+Shift+R Run all tests

Also available: test:run-file from the command palette.

docs.title: Command Palette
docs.text:
Press Ctrl+P to open the command palette. All commands are searchable. Key commands:

  • lines:sort, lines:reverse, lines:unique, lines:sort-case-insensitive
  • bookmarks:toggle, bookmarks:next, bookmarks:previous, bookmarks:clear
  • indent-guide:toggle
  • minimap:toggle
  • treeview:refresh
  • workspace:clear-project-memory, workspace:clear-recents

docs.title: Sidebar Context Menu
docs.text:
Right-click a file or folder in the sidebar:

  • Open -- open the file
  • Copy Path -- copy absolute path to clipboard
  • Copy Relative Path -- copy project-relative path
  • Refresh -- rescan the project tree
  • Rename / Delete -- file operations
  • New File / New Folder -- create in the selected directory

docs.title: LSP Support
docs.text: Lite-Anvil includes built-in configurations for the following language servers. Install the binary and it works automatically -- no configuration needed.

docs.title: Recommended Language Servers
docs.text: Language Server Install Rust rust-analyzer rustup component add rust-analyzer Python pyright-langserver pip install pyright or npm install -g pyright Go gopls go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest JavaScript / TypeScript / TSX typescript-language-server npm install -g typescript-language-server typescript C / C++ clangd Package manager (e.g. apt install clangd) Java jdtls Eclipse JDT.LS Kotlin kotlin-language-server GitHub releases C# OmniSharp dotnet tool install -g OmniSharp F# fsautocomplete dotnet tool install -g fsautocomplete Scala metals Metals PHP intelephense npm install -g intelephense Ruby ruby-lsp gem install ruby-lsp Lua lua-language-server GitHub releases Bash bash-language-server npm install -g bash-language-server Zig zls ZLS Haskell haskell-language-server ghcup install hls Elixir elixir-ls ElixirLS Erlang erlang_ls erlang_ls OCaml ocamllsp opam install ocaml-lsp-server Gleam gleam lsp Bundled with gleam CLI Dart dart language-server Bundled with Dart SDK Swift sourcekit-lsp Bundled with Swift toolchain Julia LanguageServer.jl julia -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.add("LanguageServer")' Clojure clojure-lsp GitHub releases Crystal crystalline Crystalline

docs.title: Custom LSP Configuration
docs.text:
Create lsp.json in your user config directory or project root to add servers or override builtins:

{
  "my_server": {
    "command": ["my-lsp", "--stdio"],
    "filetypes": ["mylang"],
    "rootPatterns": ["myproject.toml", ".git"]
  }
}

Set "autostart": false to disable a builtin server.

docs.title: Syntax Highlighting
docs.text:
51 built-in syntax grammars:

Category Languages Systems Assembly, C, C++, D, Rust, Zig JVM Java, Kotlin, Scala, Groovy, Clojure Web JavaScript, TypeScript, TSX, HTML, CSS, Vue, Svelte, PHP .NET C#, F# Scripting Python, Ruby, Lua, Bash, PowerShell, R, Lisp Functional Haskell, OCaml, Elixir, Erlang, Gleam, Julia, Crystal Other Go, Dart, Swift Data/Config JSON (via JS), TOML, YAML, INI, XML, CSV, Env, Fstab, SQL, PostgreSQL, Meson Markup Markdown, Dockerfile

XML highlighting also covers .csproj, .fsproj, .vbproj, .vcxproj, .sln, .props, .targets, .nuspec, .pom, .svg, .plist, .xaml.

Groovy highlighting covers .gradle files. Kotlin highlighting covers .gradle.kts files (via .kts extension).

docs.title: Test Runner
docs.text:
The test runner auto-detects your project's framework and runs tests in a terminal pane.

Language Detection Tool Run All Run File Rust Cargo.toml cargo cargo test cargo test <module> JavaScript / TypeScript package.json vitest, jest, or npm npx vitest run / npx jest / npm test <runner> <file> Python pytest.ini, conftest.py, pyproject.toml pytest or unittest python -m pytest -v pytest <file> Go go.mod go test go test ./... go test -v ./<dir> C# / F# .sln, .csproj, .fsproj dotnet test dotnet test dotnet test --filter <class> Java / Kotlin / Scala build.gradle[.kts] Gradle ./gradlew test ./gradlew test --tests "*Class*" Java / Kotlin / Scala pom.xml Maven mvn test mvn test -Dtest="Class" Scala build.sbt sbt sbt test sbt "testOnly *Class*" PHP phpunit.xml[.dist] PHPUnit ./vendor/bin/phpunit phpunit <file> Any Makefile make make test --

Configure a custom command: set config.plugins.test_runner.custom_command in config.lua.

docs.title: Plugins
docs.text: Lite-Anvil is extensible via Lua plugins. All core modules are Rust, but user plugins and configuration use Lua.

docs.title: Plugin locations
docs.text:
Platform Path Linux ~/.config/lite-anvil/plugins/ macOS ~/Library/Application Support/lite-anvil/plugins/ Windows %APPDATA%\lite-anvil\plugins\

Each plugin is a single .lua file or a directory with an init.lua.

docs.title: Mod-version
docs.text:
Every plugin must declare its compatible API version. The current mod-version is 4.0.0.

-- mod-version:4

docs.title: Example plugin
docs.text:
-- mod-version:4
local core = require "core"
local command = require "core.command"
local keymap = require "core.keymap"

command.add(nil, {
  ["hello:say-hello"] = function()
    core.log("Hello from my plugin!")
  end,
})

keymap.add {
  ["ctrl+shift+h"] = "hello:say-hello",
}

docs.title: Disabling plugins
docs.text:
In config.lua or init.lua:

local config = require "core.config"
config.plugins.minimap = false
config.plugins.drawwhitespace = false

docs.title: Available APIs
docs.text:
Plugins can use any module via require:

  • core -- logging, open_doc, projects, active_view
  • core.command -- register and perform commands
  • core.keymap -- bind keys to commands
  • core.config -- read/write editor settings
  • core.style -- colors and fonts
  • core.doc -- document model (lines, selections, edits)
  • core.docview -- document rendering (override draw methods)
  • core.view -- base view class for custom views
  • core.common -- path utilities, drawing helpers
  • core.syntax -- register custom syntax grammars

See PLUGINS_GUIDE.md for the full API reference.

docs.title: Prebuilt Binaries
docs.text: Download the latest release for your platform from GitHub Releases.

docs.title: Linux
docs.text:
Extract the archive, copy the binary and data directory:

cp lite-anvil ~/.local/bin/
cp -r data ~/.local/share/lite-anvil/

Optional -- register for "Open With" on supported file types:

cp resources/linux/com.lite_anvil.LiteAnvil.desktop ~/.local/share/applications/
cp resources/icons/lite-anvil.png ~/.local/share/icons/hicolor/128x128/apps/
update-desktop-database ~/.local/share/applications/

docs.title: macOS
docs.text:
1. Download the .app bundle or build one (see below).
  2. Move LiteAnvil.app to /Applications.
  3. Sign the bundle so macOS doesn't block it:
codesign --force --deep --sign - --timestamp=none /Applications/LiteAnvil.app

If macOS still refuses to open it (Gatekeeper quarantine), remove the quarantine attribute:

sudo xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/LiteAnvil.app

docs.title: Windows
docs.text:
Extract the archive. Copy lite-anvil.exe and the data/ directory wherever you like.

Optional -- register file associations:

powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File resources\windows\install-file-associations.ps1

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Rust 1.85+ via rustup
  • System libraries:
Library Ubuntu/Debian Fedora Arch macOS (Homebrew) SDL3 libsdl3-dev SDL3-devel sdl3 sdl3 FreeType2 libfreetype6-dev freetype-devel freetype2 freetype PCRE2 libpcre2-dev pcre2-devel pcre2 pcre2

Lua 5.4 is not required -- it is vendored by the mlua crate.

docs.title: Build & Run
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/danpozmanter/lite-anvil.git
cd lite-anvil
cargo build --release
./target/release/lite-anvil [path]

docs.title: macOS App Bundle (from source)
docs.text:
mkdir -p LiteAnvil.app/Contents/MacOS
cp target/release/lite-anvil LiteAnvil.app/Contents/MacOS/
cp -r data LiteAnvil.app/Contents/MacOS/
cp resources/macos/Info.plist LiteAnvil.app/Contents/
codesign --force --deep --sign - --timestamp=none LiteAnvil.app

docs.title: Debian Package
docs.text:
cargo install cargo-deb
cargo deb --no-build -p forge-core

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
User config location:

Platform Path Linux ~/.config/lite-anvil/ macOS ~/Library/Application Support/lite-anvil/ Windows %APPDATA%\lite-anvil\

Key files:

  • config.lua -- editor settings, keybindings, plugin config
  • init.lua -- user initialization script
  • plugins/ -- user Lua plugins (see Plugins)
  • lsp.json -- custom LSP server configurations (see LSP Support)
